loopStyle: Async turn loop (Engine::submit_streaming — loop{} over provider.stream); read-only tools run in parallel, writes sequentially, each behind a 4-mode permission gate (default / accept-edits / bypass / plan)

nodes.label: Submit user message
nodes.note: **submit_streaming** first runs **maybe_auto_compact** (query.rs:536), then pushes a **Message::user** onto the conversation. The REPL/TUI calls this per turn; events stream back to the UI over an mpsc channel.

nodes.label: Turn loop (loop {})
nodes.note: The **turn loop**: `loop {}` that keeps going as long as the assistant requests tools. Each iteration rebuilds tool defs (**tools.definitions**, 9 built-ins + MCP) and starts a fresh model stream. Has built-in recovery: prompt-too-long → compact & retry (up to 3×), max-output-tokens → double max_tokens (query.rs:606).

nodes.label: provider.stream
nodes.note: **provider.stream** opens one streaming generation (Anthropic / OpenAI-compatible, api/provider.rs) and returns an **mpsc::Receiver<ApiEvent>**. One call = one assistant turn; it does not auto-loop tools.

nodes.label: Drain ApiEvent stream
nodes.note: `while let Some(event) = rx.recv()` folds events: **Text** → stream to UI + buffer; **ToolUse** → push (id,name,input) + emit ToolStart; **Usage** → cost tracking; **Done** → break. Collected text + tool-use blocks become one **assistant message** (query.rs:646).

nodes.label: PermissionChecker.check
nodes.note: Per tool-use, **permissions.check(name, input, is_read_only)** returns **Allow / Deny / Ask** under one of four modes — **Default** (ask writes + Read/Grep/WebFetch, auto-allow Glob), **AcceptEdits** (auto Write/Edit, ask Bash), **Bypass** (allow all), **Plan** (allow reads, deny all writes). Session `always_allow` overrides. An **Ask** sends a PermissionRequest(+diff) to the UI (query.rs:675); the TUI answers **y/Enter→Allow, a→AlwaysAllow, n/Esc→Deny** (chat.rs:501).

nodes.label: Run tool (read-only ∥, writes →)
nodes.note: On **Allow**, **tools.execute** runs the tool. In the non-streaming path, read-only + auto-allowed tools are partitioned out and run **concurrently** (execute_tools_parallel, query.rs:425) while writes run sequentially; the streaming path runs them in order. Output is truncated to protect context (compact::truncate_tool_output).

nodes.label: Permission denied
nodes.note: A mode **Deny** (e.g. Plan mode write) yields `Permission denied: <reason>` (query.rs:671); a user **Deny** at the prompt yields `Permission denied by user.` (query.rs:726). Either way the tool does **not** run — an **error tool-result** is recorded and fed back to the model, so the loop continues.

nodes.label: Append tool results
nodes.note: All result blocks (successes + denials, truncated) are pushed as one **Message::tool_results**. Control returns to the top of the turn loop, which re-prompts the model with the results in context.

nodes.label: Turn complete
nodes.note: When the assistant turn produced **no tool-use blocks**, the loop emits **StreamEvent::Done** and **breaks** (query.rs:650) — the final text is the answer. The non-streaming `submit` mirrors this at query.rs:409.

scenarios.title: Model answers with **no tools**
scenarios.note: The stream yields only **Text** and **Done**; `tool_uses` is empty, so the loop emits StreamEvent::Done and breaks on the first iteration. The buffered text is the response.

scenarios.title: User asks to **edit a file**
scenarios.note: The model emits an **Edit** tool-use. In **Default** mode permissions.check returns **Ask** with a generated diff (permissions.rs:176); the UI shows the diff and the user presses **y**. Edit runs, its result is appended, the loop re-prompts, and the model finishes with no further tools. (In **AcceptEdits** mode the Edit auto-allows with no prompt.)

scenarios.title: **Tool call is denied**
scenarios.note: Either the user presses **n** at the prompt, or the harness is in **Plan** mode and the model called a write tool → permissions.check returns **Deny** (permissions.rs:103). No execution happens; a `Permission denied…` error tool-result is fed back, and the model adapts on the next turn and finishes.

scenarios.title: Model issues **several tool calls**
scenarios.note: One assistant turn carries multiple tool-use blocks. Each is permission-checked independently; read-only + auto-allowed calls (e.g. two Reads) can run **in parallel** (execute_tools_parallel, query.rs:425) while writes run sequentially. All results are appended in original order as a single tool_results message, then the model is re-prompted and finishes.
